abstract: A comparison of soluble protein, esterase, GDH and ADH isoenzyme patterns in seeds of different steriles, maintainers and restorer lines exhibited similarities as well as differences. Soluble protein patterns from sterile and maintainer lines differed both qualitatively and quantitatively. Based on the esterase patterns, male steriles with different cytoplasms could be separated into three groups (i) Ck 60A and B; Nagpur A and B, (ii) M 35-1A and 1 B, M 31-2A and 2B, (iii) G1A and B, VZM2A and 2B. Each group could further be differentiated on the basis of minor differences in esterase isoenzyme patterns within each group. ADH and GDH patterns in general were similar in both sterile and maintainer lines.
affiliation: Nuclear Research Laboratory, Indian Agricultural Research Institute (New Delhi)
National Research Centre for Sorghum IARI-Regional Station (Hyderabad)
keywords: Isoenzyme, Male sterile (A), Maintainer (B), Restorer (R), Apomict, Sorghum bicolor (L.), Moench, Gel electrophoresis
note:
The authors are grateful to Dr. B. R. Murty, the Project
Director, Nuclear Research Laboratory, for providing the
research facilities.
title: Soluble proteins and isoenzymes from seeds of diverse male steriles of sorghum, (Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench)
